title: FOB vs DDP: Choosing the Right Shipping Term for Resort Wear
excerpt: FOB works well for brands with freight partners. DDP gives newer teams one landed price with duties, customs, and delivery included.
author: Aloha and Co

sections.title: When FOB Makes Sense
paragraphs: FOB is useful when a brand already has a freight forwarder and wants direct control over international shipping, consolidation, customs, and final delivery.

sections.title: Why Many Startups Choose DDP
paragraphs: DDP is often simpler for emerging brands because the quote includes tariff, customs handling, and door-to-door delivery. That makes landed cost easier to understand before production starts.

sections.title: Quote Before Production
paragraphs: The best choice depends on destination, order size, timing, and the brand's internal logistics setup. Confirm shipping terms with the production quote so margin math stays clear.
